'''Happer''' was a [[Mos Eisley Police Authority|Mos Eisley police]] [[Police officer/Legends|guard]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biography==
He was on duty when the [[Tonnika sisters/Legends|Tonnika sisters]] were being detained at his [[Mos Eisley Police Station|facility's]] jail cells before being called away to an incident in [[Docking Bay 94/Legends|Docking Bay 94]] to back up the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] presence there.

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> he was tasked to investigate the murder of the [[Mos Eisley Biolab|lab]] assistant [[Bera Jeza]].<ref name="SWG:GC" />

==Behind the scenes==
In his rush to leave, Happer mistakenly told [[Riij Winward]] that there's trouble "brewing over at Docking Bay 97".

Happer appeared as a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]] (NPC) in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:GC" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Happer was added to the game with the "[[W:c:swg:Game Update 13|Game Update 13]]," released on [[September 30]], [[2009]].<ref>{{SonyForumsCite|url=swg/posts/list.m?topic_id=1003113|text=''Star Wars'' Galaxies: Game Update 13 -- Chronicle Master System}}</ref> This NPC was part of the questline "[[W:c:swg:Something is Obviously Wrong|Something is Obviously Wrong]],"<ref name="SWG:GC" /> an introduction to the "[[W:c:swg:Death Troopers|Death Troopers]]" [[W:c:swg:Game Update 14|update]].<ref name="SonyForumsCite-Game Update 14: Death Troopers">{{SonyForumsCite|url=swg/posts/list.m?topic_id=1008376|text=Game Update 14: Death Troopers}}</ref>
